briefing: Amazon's chip business reaches $20 billion, marking a significant expansion beyond initial offerings.
Google Cloud reports $20 billion in revenue but cites growth limitations due to capacity constraints.
OpenAI partners with Amazon Bedrock, enhancing its model availability and cloud integration.
BrandJet AI launches Artemis, an automation agent designed to streamline sales workflows.

articles.title: Amazon chips no longer just a side dish, they're a $20B biz
articles.snippet: The Trainium train keeps a-rollin' Amazon is now among the top three datacenter chip businesses in the world, as its semiconductor business surpassed a $20 billion annual run rate ... and it would be closer to $50 billion if it included itself among the customers, CEO Andy Jassy said during the company’s first quarter earnings call on Wednesday.…
articles.ai_summary: Amazon's semiconductor business has surpassed a $20 billion annual run rate, positioning it among the top three datacenter chip companies globally. CEO Andy Jassy indicated that the revenue could approach $50 billion if Amazon included itself as a customer.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights Amazon's growing influence in the semiconductor industry.

articles.title: How Elon Musk Squeezed OpenAI: They 'Are Gonna Want to Kill Me’

articles.title: Extracting contract insights with PwC’s AI-driven annotation on AWS
articles.snippet: This post was co-written with Yash Munsadwala, Adam Hood, Justin Guse, and Hector Hernandez from PwC. Contract analysis often consumes significant time for legal, compliance, and procurement teams, especially when important insights are buried in lengthy, unstructured agreements. As contract volumes grow, finding specific clauses and assessing extracted terms can become increasingly difficult to scale. […]
articles.ai_summary: PwC has developed an AI-driven annotation tool on AWS to streamline contract analysis, helping legal and compliance teams extract insights from lengthy documents more efficiently.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances efficiency in legal processes through AI technology.

articles.title: Researchers move in the right direction, develop powerful GPS interference alarm
articles.snippet: ORNL says portable detector kit can separate real GPS signals from fake ones even at equal strength GPS spoofing, which sends fake satellite-like signals, and GPS jamming, which drowns receivers in noise, are increasingly serious problems. Researchers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee have created what they say is the most effective system yet for detecting GPS interference, which could help blunt such attacks.…
articles.ai_summary: Researchers at Oak Ridge National Laboratory have developed a portable detector kit that can effectively identify real GPS signals from spoofed ones, addressing serious concerns about GPS interference.
articles.why_it_matters: Advances efforts to enhance security against GPS-related threats.
